excel_structure.notes: Excel file contains a single test case with 10 test steps covering user profile management functionality including viewing, editing, and updating profile information with validation scenarios

test_case_metadata.title: Verify User Profile Management
test_case_metadata.description: Test case to verify that users can view and edit their profile information successfully

preconditions.raw_text: User must be logged in to the application
preconditions.interpreted_meaning: Before executing this test case, the user must have successfully authenticated and established an active session in the application. This requires valid credentials, successful login flow completion, and an active session token or cookie.

implied_components.reason: Need login page to authenticate user before accessing profile
Need username field locator for login precondition
Need password field locator for login precondition
Need login button locator to submit credentials
Need a method that performs the complete login action to satisfy precondition
Need valid username and password to login before profile access
Need to verify that login was successful before proceeding to profile tests

test_steps.raw_expected_result: User Profile page is displayed with user information
test_steps.interpreted_action: This step requires navigating to the user profile page from the current logged-in state. This could be done by clicking a profile link/icon in the navigation menu or header, or by directly accessing the profile URL. The system should load the profile page and display the current user's profile information including fields like name, email, phone, address, and other profile attributes.

sub_actions.action: Click on profile navigation link or icon
Wait for profile page to load

ui_elements_involved.element_identifier_hint: Profile link in navigation menu — likely has text 'Profile', 'My Profile', or 'Account' or icon with aria-label='Profile'
Main container for profile page — likely has class='profile-container' or id='profile-page'
Display element showing user's name — likely has class='user-name' or id='userName'
Display element showing user's email — likely has class='user-email' or id='userEmail'

navigation_involved.expected_page_indicator: Profile page container is visible with user information displayed

assertions.expected_value: Profile page is loaded and visible
assertions.assertion_description: Verify that the user profile page has loaded successfully and the main profile container is visible
Verify that user name is displayed on the profile page
Verify that user email is displayed on the profile page

wait_conditions.wait_description: Wait for the profile page to fully load after navigation
Wait for profile page container to be visible

error_scenarios.error_description: Profile page does not load — timeout or navigation failure
error_scenarios.error_indicator: Profile page container not found within timeout
Redirected to login page instead of profile page
error_scenarios.impact: Precondition not met — test cannot proceed

dependencies.dependency_detail: Depends on precondition that user is logged in

api_calls_implied.purpose: Fetch user profile data to display on the page

test_steps.automation_notes_for_step: Standard navigation step. Ensure proper wait for page load and data population. Profile link locator may vary based on UI design.

test_steps.raw_action: Verify that all profile fields are displayed correctly
test_steps.raw_expected_result: All profile fields (Name, Email, Phone, Address) are visible and contain correct data
test_steps.interpreted_action: This step requires verifying that all expected profile fields are present on the page and visible to the user. The fields mentioned are Name, Email, Phone, and Address. Each field should be checked for visibility and should contain the correct data for the logged-in user. This is a comprehensive validation step that ensures data integrity and proper UI rendering.

Verify Email field is visible
Verify Phone field is visible
Verify Address field is visible
Verify Name field contains correct data
Verify Email field contains correct data
Verify Phone field contains correct data
Verify Address field contains correct data

Email field display — likely has label 'Email' or 'Email Address' with associated value display element
Phone field display — likely has label 'Phone' or 'Phone Number' with associated value display element
Address field display — likely has label 'Address' with associated value display element

test_data_needed.constraints: Must match the name of the logged-in user
Must match the email of the logged-in user
Must match the phone number of the logged-in user
Must match the address of the logged-in user

Verify that the Email field is visible on the profile page
Verify that the Phone field is visible on the profile page

Verify that the Name field contains the correct user name

Verify that the Phone field contains the correct user phone number

error_scenarios.error_indicator: Field element not found or not visible
error_scenarios.impact: Data display issue — profile page not rendering correctly

error_scenarios.error_description: Profile data does not match expected values
error_scenarios.error_indicator: Text content mismatch in field values
error_scenarios.impact: Data integrity issue — incorrect user data displayed

dependencies.dependency_detail: Depends on step 1 to have navigated to profile page successfully

test_steps.automation_notes_for_step: This is a comprehensive verification step with multiple assertions. Consider creating a reusable method to verify all profile fields at once. Expected data should be retrieved from test data setup or API.

test_steps.raw_expected_result: Profile edit mode is enabled and fields become editable
test_steps.interpreted_action: This step requires locating and clicking the Edit Profile button on the profile page. After clicking, the page should transition to edit mode where the profile fields that were previously read-only become editable input fields. This may involve UI changes such as text displays becoming input fields, and the appearance of Save and Cancel buttons.

sub_actions.action: Click Edit Profile button
Wait for edit mode to activate

ui_elements_involved.element_identifier_hint: Edit Profile button — likely has text 'Edit Profile', 'Edit', or icon with aria-label='Edit Profile'
Name input field in edit mode — likely has id='name' or name='name' or placeholder='Enter name'
Email input field in edit mode — likely has id='email' or name='email' or type='email'
Phone input field in edit mode — likely has id='phone' or name='phone' or type='tel'
Address input field in edit mode — likely has id='address' or name='address'

navigation_involved.expected_page_indicator: Profile fields become editable input fields

assertions.assertion_description: Verify that the Name field becomes editable after clicking Edit Profile
Verify that the Email field becomes editable after clicking Edit Profile
Verify that the Phone field becomes editable after clicking Edit Profile
Verify that the Address field becomes editable after clicking Edit Profile

wait_conditions.wait_description: Wait for name input field to become enabled after clicking Edit Profile

error_scenarios.error_description: Edit Profile button not found or not clickable
error_scenarios.error_indicator: Button element not found or disabled

test_steps.automation_notes_for_step: Standard button click with state change verification. Ensure proper wait for UI transition from display mode to edit mode.

test_steps.raw_action: Update the Name field with new value
test_steps.raw_expected_result: Name field is updated with the new value
test_steps.interpreted_action: This step requires clearing the existing value in the Name input field and entering a new name value 'John Smith'. The field should accept the input and display the new value. This is a data entry step that modifies the profile information.

sub_actions.action: Clear existing value in Name field
Type new name value

ui_elements_involved.element_identifier_hint: Name input field in edit mode — likely has id='name' or name='name'

test_data_needed.constraints: Should be a valid name format
test_data_needed.used_for: Updating the name field in profile

assertions.assertion_description: Verify that the Name field contains the newly entered value 'John Smith'

error_scenarios.error_indicator: Field remains empty or shows old value after typing
error_scenarios.impact: Cannot update name — data entry failure

dependencies.dependency_detail: Depends on step 3 to have enabled edit mode

test_steps.automation_notes_for_step: Standard text input step. Ensure field is cleared before entering new value to avoid appending to existing value.

test_steps.raw_action: Update the Phone field with new value
test_steps.raw_expected_result: Phone field is updated with the new value
test_steps.interpreted_action: This step requires clearing the existing value in the Phone input field and entering a new phone number '+1987654321'. The field should accept the input and display the new value. This is a data entry step that modifies the phone number in the profile.

Type new phone value

test_steps.automation_notes_for_step: Standard text input step. Phone field may have format validation or masking. Ensure the entered value matches expected format.

test_steps.raw_action: Click Save button to save changes
test_steps.raw_expected_result: Changes are saved successfully and success message is displayed
test_steps.interpreted_action: This step requires locating and clicking the Save button to persist the profile changes made in previous steps. After clicking, the system should save the updated profile information to the backend, display a success message confirming the save operation, and potentially transition back to view mode showing the updated information.

sub_actions.action: Click Save button
Wait for save operation to complete

ui_elements_involved.element_identifier_hint: Save button — likely has text 'Save', 'Save Changes', 'Update Profile' or id='saveBtn'
Success message element — likely has class='success-message' or 'alert-success' with text like 'Profile updated successfully'

assertions.assertion_description: Verify that a success message is displayed after saving profile changes
Verify that the success message contains text indicating successful save operation

wait_conditions.wait_description: Wait for success message to appear after clicking Save

test_steps.raw_action: Verify that updated values are displayed in profile
test_steps.raw_expected_result: Profile displays updated Name and Phone values
test_steps.interpreted_action: This step requires verifying that after the save operation, the profile page displays the updated values for Name and Phone fields. This confirms that the changes were successfully persisted and are now reflected in the UI. The page may have transitioned back to view mode, so the values should be displayed in read-only format.

Verify Phone field displays updated value

ui_elements_involved.element_identifier_hint: Name field display in view mode — shows the updated name value
Phone field display in view mode — shows the updated phone value

Must match the value entered in step 5

assertions.assertion_description: Verify that the Name field displays the updated value 'John Smith'
Verify that the Phone field displays the updated value '+1987654321'

test_steps.raw_action: Clear the Phone field and click Save
test_steps.raw_expected_result: Validation error is displayed indicating Phone is required
test_steps.interpreted_action: This step tests field validation by clearing the Phone field (making it empty) and attempting to save. The system should detect that the Phone field is required and display a validation error message preventing the save operation. This is a negative test scenario to verify that required field validation is working correctly.

sub_actions.action: Clear the Phone field
Click Save button
Wait for validation error to appear

ui_elements_involved.element_identifier_hint: Phone input field in edit mode
Save button
Validation error message for Phone field — likely has class='error-message' or 'field-error' near phone field with text like 'Phone is required'

assertions.assertion_description: Verify that a validation error message is displayed for the Phone field

assertions.expected_value: required or mandatory or cannot be empty
assertions.assertion_description: Verify that the validation error message indicates Phone field is required

wait_conditions.wait_description: Wait for validation error message to appear after clicking Save

error_scenarios.error_indicator: No error message displayed after clearing required field and saving
error_scenarios.impact: Required field validation not working — data integrity risk

error_scenarios.error_description: Save succeeds with empty Phone field
error_scenarios.error_indicator: Success message displayed instead of validation error
error_scenarios.impact: Critical validation failure — required field can be saved empty

dependencies.dependency_detail: Depends on step 8 to have re-entered edit mode

test_steps.automation_notes_for_step: Negative test scenario for validation. Ensure that validation error is properly detected and save operation is prevented.

test_steps.raw_expected_result: Edit mode is cancelled and profile returns to view mode with original values
test_steps.interpreted_action: This step requires clicking the Cancel button to exit edit mode without saving changes. The system should discard any unsaved changes and return the profile page to view mode, displaying the original values that were present before entering edit mode. This tests the cancel functionality and ensures that unsaved changes are not persisted.

sub_actions.action: Click Cancel button
Wait for view mode to activate

ui_elements_involved.element_identifier_hint: Cancel button — likely has text 'Cancel', 'Discard Changes', or 'Close' or id='cancelBtn'
Name field display in view mode
Phone field display in view mode

test_data_needed.constraints: Should be the phone value that was saved in step 6
test_data_needed.used_for: Verification that original values are restored after cancel

assertions.assertion_description: Verify that profile returns to view mode with display fields visible
Verify that Phone field displays the original value (not the cleared value) after cancelling
Verify that validation error is no longer displayed after cancelling

wait_conditions.wait_description: Wait for profile to return to view mode after clicking Cancel

test_steps.automation_notes_for_step: Tests cancel functionality and ensures unsaved changes are discarded. Important to verify that original values are restored and validation errors are cleared.

postconditions.interpreted_meaning: After test execution, the user profile should be in a consistent state with the updated values (Name: John Smith, Phone: +1987654321) saved from step 6. The user should be logged out to clean up the session, and any test data created should be cleaned up if necessary.

flow_sequence.action_summary: User logs in to the application (precondition)
Enter edit mode and update profile fields
Test validation by clearing required field
Cancel changes and verify original values restored

flow_analysis.critical_path: Login → Navigate to Profile → View Profile → Edit Profile → Update Name and Phone → Save → Verify Updates

alternate_paths.description: When required field is cleared and save is attempted, validation error is displayed and save is prevented
When cancel is clicked, unsaved changes are discarded and profile returns to view mode with original values

error_paths.trigger: Navigation to profile page fails or times out
Backend API fails to save profile changes

teardown_requirements: Profile data may need to be reset to original state for test repeatability

automation_notes.complexity_justification: This test case involves multiple interactions including navigation, form editing, data validation, and state transitions between view and edit modes. It includes positive test scenarios (successful profile update) and negative test scenarios (validation error testing). The test requires handling dynamic UI state changes and verifying data persistence. However, it does not involve complex elements like iframes, multi-window handling, or file uploads, keeping the complexity at medium level.

risks.risk: Dynamic element IDs or classes may change between releases requiring locator updates
risks.mitigation: Use data-testid attributes or stable CSS selectors. Implement locator abstraction layer in page objects for easy maintenance.

risks.risk: Test data dependency — test requires specific user profile data to exist and may be affected by previous test runs
risks.mitigation: Use API-based test data setup to create fresh user with known profile data before each test run. Implement proper test data cleanup in teardown.

risks.risk: Timing issues with UI state transitions between view and edit modes may cause flaky test failures
risks.mitigation: Use explicit waits with proper conditions for element state changes (visible, enabled, text content). Avoid fixed delays.

risks.risk: Validation error messages may vary in wording or format making text-based assertions brittle
risks.mitigation: Use partial text matching or regex patterns for validation error assertions. Consider using data-testid on error elements.

risks.risk: Profile save operation may involve asynchronous API calls with variable response times across environments
risks.mitigation: Implement robust wait strategies for success message appearance. Consider adding API response monitoring for better debugging.

recommendations: Implement Page Object Model pattern with ProfilePage class containing separate methods for view mode and edit mode interactions
Create a reusable profile verification method that can validate all fields at once to reduce code duplication
Use API calls for test data setup (creating user with known profile data) and teardown (resetting profile to original state) to ensure test repeatability
Implement proper wait strategies using explicit waits for all UI state transitions and element interactions
Add screenshot capture on assertion failures for debugging, especially for validation error scenarios
Make all test data configurable through test data files or environment variables to support multi-environment testing
Consider parameterizing the profile update test to test multiple field combinations and validation scenarios
Implement retry logic for network-dependent operations like save and page load
Add logging for each major step to aid in debugging test failures
Consider adding API-level verification after UI save operation to confirm data persistence at backend level
special_handling_notes: The test involves state transitions between view and edit modes which require careful handling of element locators as the same data may be displayed in different element types (text display vs input field)
Validation testing in step 9 is a negative scenario that expects an error — ensure the test framework properly handles expected failures
The cancel functionality in step 10 requires verification that changes are discarded, which may need comparison with values from earlier steps or API data
Phone field may have format validation or input masking that could affect how data is entered and displayed
Success message after save may be transient (auto-dismiss) requiring quick verification before it disappears
automation_notes.framework_compatibility_notes: This test case analysis is structured to be compatible with the Aura framework's page object model and utility patterns. All suggested component names follow the framework's naming conventions (snake_case for elements and methods, PascalCase for page objects). The test flow supports both UI-based and API-based approaches for test data management, which aligns with modern test automation best practices.
